title: AI Meets Gold Lending: Wizz Financial Introduces Next-Gen Lending Experience
Business News | AI Meets Gold Lending: Wizz Financial Introduces Next-Gen Lending Experience
description: Get latest articles and stories on Business at LatestLY. Kochi (Kerala) [India], April 9: Gold loans in India have long been trusted for quick access to credit. The process, however, remains largely manual, time-bound and operationally intensive. Wizzmoni, a flagship brand of Wizz Financial, is now redefining that experience.

title: Here’s how Google’s AI can help you shop across Gemini, Search and more
description: New Delhi: Google has introduced a set of AI-powered shopping features in India across its Gemini app, AI Mode in Search and Circle to Search, expanding how users discover and compare products online. The updates build on Google’s broader shopping infrastructure, including its Shopping Graph, which contains data on billions of products and is updated frequently to reflect pricing and availability. In the Gemini app, users can now move from general queries to product browsing within the same chat. The app provides product listings, price comparisons and links to purchase items from different websites. It also supports follow-up queries, allowing users to refine results within a single conversation. The feature is currently available in English and Hindi. Here’s how to use these features: Users can begin by opening the Gemini app and typing a natural query, such as looking for outfit ideas or gift suggestions. The app will generate product recommendations along with prices and links to buy. In Google Search’s AI Mode, users can ask shopping-related questions to view organised results, including images, pricing and comparison tables. For Circle to Search, users can tap, circle or scribble over items on their screen, such as clothing or gadgets, to identify products and find similar options online. In AI Mode within Google Search, the company has introduced a more structured way to browse products. When users enter shopping-related queries, the system presents organised results that include images, pricing details and availability. It can also generate side-by-side comparisons of products, drawing on information from reviews to highlight differences. This feature is available in English, with Hindi support expected to follow. Google has also updated Circle to Search on select devices, including Pixel 10 and Samsung Galaxy S26 series smartphones. The feature allows users to identify multiple objects on their screen by circling, tapping or scribbling over them. The system then finds similar items online, enabling users to explore or compare products directly. The company said shopping-related queries are among the more common uses of Circle to Search, particularly for identifying fashion items and similar products seen on social media or other platforms.

title: India deploys AI in state elections for smoother polling
description: Kerala is using an AI-powered system to monitor polling stations in real time, flag crowds and queues, marking a new step in India’s tech-driven election management.

title: Nava Series A Funding Backs AI Data Center Expansion
description: Nava (formerly Kluisz), a cloud infrastructure company building GPU compute and AI data centres across Asia-Pacific, today announced a $22 million Series A led by Greenoaks, with participation from RTP Global and Unicorn India Ventures. This raise marks a significant step in Nava’s journey to build the foundational cloud infrastructure layer for AI. Alongside the fundraise, the company also announced its rebrand to Nava. As part of its next phase of growth, Nava is establishing Singapore as its regional headquarters, positioning itself closer to key APAC markets and access to global talent. This funding will enable Nava to build out its full-stack AI compute platform, expand and scale operations across APAC, and hire senior leadership and high-caliber talent across AI data center design, GPU engineering, go-to-market, and operations. Founded in 2025 by Abhinav Sinha (Former Global COO & CPO at OYO, ex BCG), Vamshidhar Reddy (Former Partner at McKinsey, ex-AMD), and Abhijeet Singh (Former VP of Cloud at Jio, ex-AT&T), Nava is building a full-stack neo-cloud platform purpose-built for AI workloads. As enterprises rapidly adopt AI, they need next-gen hardware paired with AI-native software to deliver high performance at efficient cost. The infrastructure gap is widening. KPMG estimates that data center capacity across Southeast Asia will need to triple by 2030 to meet surging AI compute demand, and India faces a comparable shortfall. Yet most of that capacity today is legacy cloud, not purpose-built for AI workloads. Nava is adopting a vertically integrated approach that brings together AI-optimised data centres, high-performance GPU compute, AI-native orchestration and inferencing layers, and developer-friendly access and tooling. This integrated architecture will enable enterprises to build, deploy, and scale AI applications with greater efficiency, reliability, and control. Abhinav Sinha, Co-founder and CEO, Nava, said: “This fundraise marks an important step in our journey. What started as an AI-native cloud platform has now evolved into something much larger, where we are building the foundational cloud platform layer for AI in Asia. We’re grateful to our investors for their continued support and conviction as we build in a category that is growing and rapidly evolving.” Madhur Makkar, Principal at RTP Global, said: “As one of the earliest backers of the company, we have seen Abhinav, Vamshi, and Abhijeet execute with strong clarity and speed in a highly complex space. In under a year, this team has demonstrated exceptional execution and is strongly positioned to address the growing need for purpose-built AI infrastructure across Asia.” Bhaskar Majumdar, Managing Partner, Unicorn India Ventures, added, “We are witnessing a structural shift with AI driving demand for compute infra and GPUs, a critical part of the overall value chain. As a deep tech investor, we back companies across the AI value chain, and Nava fits well within this strategy. The team has demonstrated strong early growth, and we see clear demand for their cloud infrastructure offerings." Nava previously raised a $9.6 million seed round led by RTP Global, with participation from Unicorn India Ventures, Blume Founders Fund, and Climber Capital.
